{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the discrete minimum-surface problem on the unit square. Divide each edge into $q$ equal intervals, giving a $(q+1)\\times(q+1)$ grid of points and hence $q\\times q$ subsquares. Let $x_j$ denote the value of the surface at the $j$th grid point; the values at the $4q$ boundary grid points are prescribed by the given boundary function, and the remaining $(q+1)^2-4q$ values are to be determined.\n\nFor a typical subsquare $A_j$, the surface-area contribution is\n\\[\nf_j(x)\\equiv \\iint_{(x,y)\\in A_j}\\sqrt{1+\\left(\\frac{\\partial z}{\\partial x}\\right)^2+\\left(\\frac{\\partial z}{\\partial y}\\right)^2}\\,dx\\,dy.\n\\]\nApproximate the derivatives by finite differences, and show that\n\\[\nf_j(x)=\\frac{1}{q^2}\\left[1+\\frac{q^2}{2}\\left[(x_j-x_{j+q+1})^2+(x_{j+1}-x_{j+q})^2\\right]\\right]^{1/2}.\n\\tag{7.43}\n\\]"}
